steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures of each choice
steps.content: Choices A and B present a noun + finite verb (asserted, were asserting). Choice C presents a noun + comma + finite verb (had asserted). Choice D presents a noun + comma + participle (asserting). Since the main verb helped appears later in the sentence, the blank must not introduce another finite verb that would create structural ambiguity.

steps.title: Apply the comma rule for participial modifiers
steps.content: A comma separates a noun from a participial phrase modifying it. Choice D correctly uses authors, asserting that American publishers' appropriation of their work caused “deep and extensive injuries...,” where the participle modifies the noun and helped remains the sole main verb. Choices A and B introduce a second finite verb, creating ambiguity. Choice C misuses the comma before a finite verb, which doesn't follow the participial modifier rule. That matches choice D — D is correct.
